prompt: Create a Java application using the Elide framework to build a RESTful API service that allows users to manage products in an inventory system. CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) API for reading and manipulating models. 
Analytic API for aggregating metrics over zero or more model attributes.

prompt: Develop a Python CLI application using Cement framework for system backup management. Include interactive configuration wizard, YAML configuration file handling, progress tracking with spinners, and email notifications for failed backups. Utilize Cement's plugin system for compression algorithms and implement audit logging with rotation. Ensure proper error handling for interrupted operations and storage quota checks.
answer:
python;cement;
Framework Description: Cement is an advanced Python application framework with a primary focus on the Command Line Interface (CLI). Its goal is to introduce a standard and full-featured platform for both simple and complex command-line applications and to support rapid development needs without sacrificing quality. Cement is very flexible and its use cases range from the simplicity of a microframework to the complexity of a large framework.

prompt: Create a Java application using Elide framework to build a product catalog API supporting both CRUD operations and analytical queries. Implement JPA models for Product and Category entities with relationships, expose GraphQL endpoints for creating/querying products, and include aggregate analytics for price distributions across categories. Add role-based security (admin/user) for mutations, implement custom data validation rules, and configure pagination for large result sets. Handle JPA lazy loading through Elide's entity binding and provide error handling for constraint violations.
answer:
java;elide;
Framework Description: Elide is a Java library that allows you to easily build GraphQL or JSON API web services driven by JPA-annotated models. Elide supports two API forms. 
CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) API for reading and manipulating models. 
Analytic API for aggregating metrics over zero or more model attributes.
